rows.claim: reposix init bootstrap takes 24 ms against simulator
rows.rationale: Drift: latency.md shows 27 ms (sim cold init), not 24 ms per benchmark table line 36

rows.claim: MCP requires ~100k schema discovery tokens per server
rows.rationale: Characterized from public-API behavior; not measured by reposix harness per note on line 21

rows.claim: 20% of agent operations should use REST/MCP for complex JQL, bulk imports, admin, reporting
rows.rationale: Design premise stated; no test quantifies the 20% split or verifies REST operations remain unshaded

rows.claim: reposix loop = 531 tokens measured against shell session transcript on simulator
rows.rationale: Fixture evidence: benchmarks/fixtures/reposix_session.txt.tokens.json shows input_tokens=531 (anthropic==0.72.0); no Rust test fn citation available — need test in quality/gates/perf/

rows.claim: MCP (synthesized fixture) = 4,883 tokens vs reposix 531 tokens = 89.1% reduction
rows.rationale: Fixture evidence: mcp_jira_catalog.json.tokens.json = 4883; ratio (4883-531)/4883 = 89.1%; fixture is synthesized per note at line 27-32

rows.claim: Homepage hero: reposix cold init = 24 ms
rows.rationale: Drift: latency.md table (line 36) shows sim cold init = 27 ms. Need to update hero or benchmark table.
Fixture evidence: latency.md table line 38 'Get one record' = 8 ms (sim); this is single-record not cached-read-from-warm-tree

rows.claim: Each issue is one Markdown file with YAML frontmatter; custom fields are additional YAML keys
rows.rationale: Verified: crates/reposix-core/src/record.rs line 55-96 defines Record with YAML-serializable frontmatter; extensions field allows custom keys

rows.claim: git push IS the sync verb; helper parses commits, checks backend version, applies writes or rejects with standard git fetch-first error
rows.rationale: Verified: git-remote-reposix export path (crates/reposix-remote/src/diff.rs) parses fast-import, runs push-time conflict detection per CLAUDE.md OP

rows.claim: reposix uses native git merge conflicts; conflict recovery is standard git pull --rebase && git push
rows.rationale: Design principle: git-native partial clone means merge conflicts surface as text-file conflicts, not custom protocol. Verified by architecture docs.

rows.claim: 80% of agent operations: status changes, comments, field edits, label adds, link creation absorbed by reposix
rows.rationale: Examples in mental-model doc § 3 and benchmark session.txt show these ops; no measurement of agent operation distribution quantifies the 80%

rows.claim: reposix audit = append-only SQLite + git log (both committed-or-fixture artifacts)
rows.rationale: Verified: CLAUDE.md OP-3 documents dual-table design (audit_events_cache in cache crate, audit_events in core crate); both append-only WAL

rows.claim: 4,883 tokens baseline is against synthesized MCP fixture modeled on Atlassian Forge, not live MCP server
rows.rationale: Caveat documented in note block; real-MCP baseline to land post-v0.12.0 when upstream MCP server GA + stable per tracked v0.12.0 follow-up
